text.title: Docker Image Optimization (size + cache + supply chain) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the docker-optimization methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Production Dockerfile MUST use multi-stage build: a `builder` stage with compilers/SDKs and a `runtime` stage with only the binary + minimal libs.
rule.rationale: Single-stage images carry compilers + headers + cache into the runtime, inflating size and attack surface.

rule.statement: Final runtime stage MUST use `gcr.io/distroless/*`, `cgr.dev/chainguard/*`, or `scratch` (for static Go/Rust); `ubuntu:*` / `debian:*` / `alpine:*` are forbidden for production unless documented exception.
rule.rationale: Distroless/Wolfi ship with zero or near-zero CVEs and < 20MB base; full distros carry shells, package managers, and 100s of unused libs.

rule.statement: Base image references MUST be digest-pinned (`FROM image@sha256:...`); plain tags (`:latest`, `:1.0`) MUST NOT be used in production.

rationale.source: OCI image spec, supply-chain best practices

rule.rationale: Tags are mutable; a 'reproducible' build with `:latest` is anything but. Digests are the only immutable anchor.

rule.statement: Final stage MUST declare `USER` (non-zero UID) and the runtime process MUST run as that user.
rule.rationale: Containers running as root extend the host kernel attack surface; non-root is the policy default.

rule.statement: Dependency installation steps MUST use BuildKit cache mounts (`RUN --mount=type=cache`); naive `pip install` / `npm install` without cache mount loses caching across rebuilds.
rule.rationale: Cache mounts speed up rebuilds 5-10x; without them dependency layers re-download on every change to upstream files.

rule.statement: Image build pipeline MUST run `hadolint` on the Dockerfile and `trivy` / `docker scout` on the produced image, gating on HIGH/CRITICAL CVE.
rule.rationale: Lint catches Dockerfile anti-patterns; scan catches CVE drift in base layers.

rule.statement: If the image is throwaway dev-only with no production exposure, this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Optimization effort against ephemeral artifacts is waste.
